page: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DLSv5 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. This instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. The instance achieved a strong multi-core score of 93251.87 in stress-ng and 17853.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5007.33 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 140.95 GB/sec. Cryptographic capabilities are particularly robust, as evidenced by a top-tier PassMark CPU encryption score of 87240.76, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Single-core performance remains average across stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D96ls_v5 provides high compute density with a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio. It lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93251.87 and top-tier cryptographic performance. The instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance for compilation and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ls_v5 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and a balanced two-gigabytes-per-core memory configuration.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance in compilation and ray tracing benchmarks
Top-tier cryptographic performance supported by AES and SHA_NI instructions
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Best suited for multi-threaded compute workloads and database operations
